title: Role-Based Activation and Welcome Experience for New Users
story: As a new DataTools Pro user logging in for the first time, I want to see a personalized welcome guide based on my role, so that I can quickly learn how to use the product for my specific needs.
description:
Create a role-based activation experience for first-time users. When a user signs up, their role is captured from the registration form and passed to the Laravel app. On first login, a welcome modal displays role-specific tutorial content loaded from a WordPress custom post type ('tutorials').

The modal shows personalized getting-started content for each role (Salesforce Admin, Data Analyst, etc.). A 'Get Started' link in the help section allows users to re-open the welcome guide at any time. The modal includes a 'Guided Tour' button that closes the modal and initiates an interactive product tour.

Content for each role persona is customized in WordPress, allowing marketing to independently manage the activation messaging.
acceptance_criteria: User's role is captured during signup and stored
First login displays a role-specific welcome modal with tutorial content
Content is loaded from WordPress tutorials custom post type
A 'Get Started' link in the help section re-opens the welcome modal
Welcome modal includes a 'Guided Tour' button with class 'guidedtour'
Clicking 'Get Started' also closes the help sidebar
ux_changes: Role-specific welcome modal on first login. 'Get Started' link in the help section. 'Guided Tour' button in the modal. Content personalized per user role.
middleware_changes: API endpoint receives user role from signup form. Session stores role for first-login detection. WordPress REST API fetches role-specific tutorial content.
data_model_changes: User role captured and stored from the registration/signup form alongside existing Gravity Forms entry ID.
api_changes: Inbound: receives role from signup form. Outbound: fetches tutorial content from WordPress REST API by role slug.
implementation_notes: Each role maps to a WordPress tutorial post. The modal only shows on the first login (controlled by session). The Guided Tour button provides a hook for the interactive product tour feature.
comment_summary: Developer implemented role capture, first-login modal, and WordPress content integration. Product customized content for all personas and requested minor tweaks (close help sidebar, add Guided Tour button). Called it a 'Big win' for personalized activation.
